verdict_reasoning: HSPD1 (Hsp60) is a mitochondrial matrix chaperonin resident in the mitochondrial inner compartment. However, it has documented surface exposure under specific conditions: (1) as a damage-associated molecular pattern (DAMP) released during cell stress, necrosis, or ICD; (2) transient surface display on apoptotic cells and during lysosomal exocytosis in certain cell states; (3) potential TLR4/MyD88-mediated immune signaling via surface-displayed or shed forms. The NCBI summary explicitly notes it may function as a signaling molecule in the innate immune system, implying extracellular or surface-accessible roles beyond canonical mitochondrial residence. These context-dependent surface exposures and immune-signaling roles qualify it as contextual rather than strictly intracellular.
